report_name: DHS Probes Breach of Sensitive Info-Sharing Network Used for World Cup Security

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a likely initial intrusion vector targeting the HSIN web portal or the connected SharePoint server. Hunting guidance in the report suggests monitoring for exploitation attempts targeting common SharePoint paths.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a likely initial intrusion vector, where the attacker may have used compromised credentials. Hunting guidance suggests monitoring for anomalous login patterns to detect this activity.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: The extended attacker dwell time suggested the use of persistence mechanisms. Hunting guidance recommended monitoring the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) for unusual child processes, which is indicative of a web shell.

identities.name: Department of Homeland Security
identities.description: The U.S. government department whose Homeland Security Information Network (HSIN) and an associated SharePoint system were compromised in a cyberattack. The platform is used for sharing sensitive information for major events like the FIFA World Cup.

indicators.name: Suspicious SharePoint Layouts Path Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious SharePoint Layouts Path Access
id: 2b3c1d4e-5f6a-4b9c-8d7e-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects access to common SharePoint administrative paths which could indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts, as mentioned in the HSIN breach report.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/07/dhs-confirms-hackers-breached-hsin-information-sharing-platform/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/_layouts/15/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to common SharePoint administrative paths (`*/_layouts/15/*`) which could indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts.

indicators.name: Potential SharePoint Webshell Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential SharePoint Webshell Activity
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning command shells, a strong indicator of a web shell, as suggested in the HSIN breach investigation.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/07/dhs-confirms-hackers-breached-hsin-information-sharing-platform/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning command shells, which is a strong indicator of a web shell execution on a SharePoint server.

indicators.name: Anomalous Account Logon Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Account Logon Activity
id: 7d6e5f4a-3b2c-4d1e-9f8a-7b6c5d4e3f2a
status: experimental
description: Detects multiple failed login attempts from a source IP followed by a successful login, which can indicate a brute-force or password spraying attack against a web application or VPN.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/07/dhs-confirms-hackers-breached-hsin-information-sharing-platform/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.t1110
level: medium
logsource:
  category: authentication
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_fail:
    EventID: 4625
  selection_success:
    EventID: 4624
  timeframe: 10m
  condition: selection_fail | count(AuthenticationPackageName) by SourceIpAddress > 5 and selection_success
indicators.description: Detects anomalous login patterns such as multiple failed attempts followed by a success, suggesting a credential-based attack.
